Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section states SmTIIAS isolated in this work was submitted as MW916096; Sm2-ODD14 was designated SmTIIAS.

Source_Species.reason: Abstract and main text identify the source plant as redroot sage.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper repeatedly identifies the species as Salvia miltiorrhiza.

Applied_Species.reason: RNAi and expression analyses were performed in redroot sage hairy roots/plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: In vivo validation used Salvia miltiorrhiza hairy roots.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Results state Sm2-ODD14 converted CT to TIIA and 'consequently, the enzyme was designated SmTIIAS'; this expands to TIIA synthase/tanshinone IIA synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper identifies Sm2-ODD14 as a 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase, places TIIASs in the DOXC31/D4H-BX6 clade, and mentions DIOX_N/2OG-FeII_Oxy domain scanning for candidate 2-ODDs.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Sm2-ODD14/SmTIIAS is characterized in the tanshinone biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Abstract, Results, and Conclusion state it converts dihydrofuran-tanshinones to furan-tanshinones and completes furan ring formation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Discussion explicitly says SmTIIAS functions at the bottleneck step and controls production of furan-tanshinones; pathway position is downstream of earlier CYP steps.

Primary_Substrate.reason: In vitro assays show activity toward CT and also iCT; text states Sm2-ODD14 converted CT to TIIA and iCT to iTIIA.

Primary_Product.reason: Results show CT was converted to TIIA and iCT to iTIIA.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results conclude Sm2-ODD14 is a desaturase introducing a double bond between C15 and C16 to complete furan ring formation, converting CT to TIIA and iCT to iTIIA.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The enzyme directly converts CT into the final nutrient/medicinal product TIIA in vitro.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Abstract says SmTIIAS controls metabolite flux from dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinones; Discussion says it acts at the bottleneck step.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No experimental localization of Sm2-ODD14/SmTIIAS is reported in the Results. Discussion only gives a general statement that 2-ODDs are cytosolic, not a direct localization result for this enzyme.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The direct product emphasized is tanshinone IIA.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Abstract describes TIIA as a diterpene quinone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract and Discussion describe TIIA as a bioactive/medicinal constituent and discuss the furano-quinone/furan ring contribution to anti-cancer activity and DNA minor-groove binding.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Tanshinones are stated to be primarily synthesized and stored in root; SmTIIAS expression is highest in root.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi lines showed decreased TIIA, increased CT, increased methylenedihydrotanshinquinone and DTI, and reduced tanshinone I; abstract also states increased total dihydrofuran-tanshinones and decreased furan-tanshinones.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR showed highest expression in root, much lower in aerial tissues, nearly undetectable in stem and inflorescence; expression increased after ABA and SA and very limitedly by MeJA.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Salvia miltiorrhiza system was used; paper reports root-preferential expression and phytohormone responses in hairy roots.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results and methods report ABA/SA increased expression, MeJA had limited effect, optimal activity at 20°C and pH 6.5, 2OG omission abolished activity, and Fe2+/ascorbate facilitated catalysis.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches appear in the paper: transcriptome mining, E. coli expression, in vitro assays with QE/LC-MS and standards, substrate specificity tests, RT-qPCR, RNAi hairy roots with metabolite profiling, kinetics, and mutagenesis.

Omics_Data.reason: Accession numbers section lists transcriptome SRA accessions PRJNA771193 and PRJNA771195.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: RNAi shifted flux toward dihydrofuran-tanshinones and reduced furan-tanshinones; no broader growth penalties were reported, matching the statement.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper suggests identification of this enzyme paves the way to design pharmaceutical resources and that TIIA is a key effective ingredient, but it does not directly test engineering applications or breeding utility.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly state strict substrate specificity: active on CT and iCT, inactive on methylenedihydrotanshinquinone, DTI, tetrahydrotanshinone I, and 1,2-didehydrocryptotanshinone; Conclusion states it is the first enzyme characterized to dehydrogenate the dihydrofuran ring to a furan.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary point is supported by Results/Abstract/Discussion: identification among candidates, CT→TIIA conversion, root-enriched and ABA/SA-responsive expression, RNAi metabolite shifts, and bottleneck/flux-control role.

corrections.reason: No experimental localization of Sm2-ODD14/SmTIIAS is reported in the Results. Discussion only gives a general statement that 2-ODDs are cytosolic, not a direct localization result for this enzyme.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section gives SmTIIAS accession MW916096.

Source_Species.reason: SmTIIAS was isolated from redroot sage.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Salvia miltiorrhiza.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays in planta were in redroot sage hairy roots.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: In vivo tests used Salvia miltiorrhiza hairy roots.

Enzyme_Name.reason: SmTIIAS is explicitly the designated enzyme name meaning TIIA synthase/tanshinone IIA synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper identifies it as a 2-ODD in DOXC31/D4H-BX6 and describes the 2-His-1-carboxylate facial triad and 2OG-binding residues.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It catalyzes dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinone conversion.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Discussion calls it a bottleneck step in the pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: CT is the main substrate directly demonstrated in vitro.

Primary_Product.reason: TIIA is the direct product from CT.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results state it dehydrogenates/desaturates the dihydrofuran ring to convert CT to TIIA.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Abstract and Discussion support key enzyme/bottleneck and flux-control role.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No localization experiment or explicit localization for SmTIIAS is reported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TIIA is the named direct product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: TIIA is described as a diterpene quinone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states TIIA is the most abundant lipophilic constituent/effective ingredient and discusses anti-cancer relevance of the furan-containing moiety.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Abstract states it controls metabolite flux from dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinones; RNAi data support this.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR shows highest expression in root relative to aerial tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Salvia miltiorrhiza system shows root-preferential expression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: ABA and SA induction are directly reported; MeJA also had limited induction, though omitted from the JSON.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: At minimum, in vitro assay and RNAi in hairy roots were both performed and reported.

Omics_Data.reason: SRA accessions PRJNA771193 and PRJNA771195 are listed.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential engineering relevance is plausible from the findings but not directly demonstrated in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly designate Sm2-ODD14 as SmTIIAS, so they are the same core gene.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported: SmTIIAS equals Sm2-ODD14, synthesizes TIIA from CT, and controls flux toward furan-tanshinones.

corrections.reason: No localization experiment or explicit localization for SmTIIAS is reported.
